rulings.Case Title: JUAN CRUZ HINOJOSA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, July 11, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Tingue, Brown & Co.¿s motion to compel further discovery responses: Defendant¿s motion is granted as to request for production no. 17 because plaintiffs¿ response is insufficient. Defendant¿s motion is granted as to requests for admission nos. 19, 21, 23, 25, 27, 29, 31, 33, 35, 37, 39, 41, 43, 45, 47, 49, 51, 53, 55, 57, 59, 61, 63, 65, 67, 69, 71, 73, 75, 77, 79, 81, 83, 85, 87, 89, 91, 93, 95, 97, 99, 101, 103, 105, 107, 109 in that plaintiffs need to provide responses without the newly-added objections, which plaintiffs waived. Defendant¿s motion did not raise an issue with plaintiffs¿ substantive responses to those requests for admission. Defendant¿s motion is granted as to requests for admissions nos. 20, 22, 24, 26, 28, 30, 32, 34, 36, 38, 40, 42, 44, 46, 48, 50, 52, 54, 56, 58, 60, 62, 64, 66, 68, 70, 72, 74, 76, 78, 80, 82, 84, 86, 88, 90, 92, 94, 96, 98, 100, 102, 104, 106, 110, in that plaintiffs need to provide responses without the newly-added objections, which plaintiffs waived, and plaintiffs¿ substantive responses are insufficient. Defendant¿s motion is granted as to form interrogatory 17.1 because plaintiffs¿ responses were insufficient and did not specify to which requests the response pertained. Defendant¿s motion to compel is denied as to special interrogatories nos. 2 and 22 because plaintiffs¿ responses were sufficient. Plaintiffs have 15 days to serve further responses. Defendant¿s requests for sanctions are denied. The court considered plaintiffs¿ opposition. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
